title: The 10-Year Perspective
author: From: The Almanack of Naval Ravikant
content: If you want to achieve things that matter, you need a 10-year view. Most people overestimate what they can do in a year and underestimate what they can do in a decade.
action: Define a high-impact objective for the 10 year horizon.

title: Compound Knowledge
author: From: The Almanack of Naval Ravikant
content: Wealth and knowledge compound. The hardest part is the start; the best part is the exponential growth at the end.
action: Focus daily time on a skill with high compounding potential.

title: Specific Knowledge
author: From: The Almanack of Naval Ravikant
content: Specific knowledge is found by pursuing your genuine curiosity rather than following what is 'hot' in the market.
action: Audit your interests to identify 'play' that functions as high-value 'work'.

title: Leverage
author: From: The Almanack of Naval Ravikant
content: There are four types of leverage: Labor, Capital, Code, and Media. Code and Media are 'permissionless'—they work while you sleep.
action: Convert current operational tasks into permissionless, scalable assets.

title: Productize Yourself
author: From: The Almanack of Naval Ravikant
content: You should be known for your unique combination of skills. That is your competitive advantage.
action: Articulate the unique intersection of skills that constitutes your professional moat.
